reason: The text describes an upcoming supernatural destruction by Yahweh that will affect the general public. The narrative establishes that Israel is currently experiencing oppression by Midian (a widespread, non-individual destruction affecting crops, livestock, and the entire population). However, the subsequent portions detail Yahweh's call to Gideon: 'Go in this might of yours and you shall save Israel from the hand of Midian' (וְהוֹשַׁעְתָּ אֶת־ יִשְׂרָאֵל מִכַּף מִדְיָן), and 'I will be with you and you shall strike Midian as one man' (כִּי אֶהְיֶה עִמָּךְ וְהִכִּיתָ אֶת־ מִדְיָן כְּאִישׁ אֶחָד). This establishes a forthcoming supernatural destruction of Midian by Yahweh's power—a significant disaster affecting not an individual but an entire enemy army and populace, which the narrative then fulfills through Gideon's divinely empowered victory.
